APIKeyCreateIn.description:
Request body for creating a new API key.

The CHECK constraint on the DB enforces scope coherence; we mirror it in
Pydantic for fast client-side feedback. The router pre-validates that the
actor can actually issue at the requested scope (super_admin → org,
team_admin → team, team member → project).
APIKeyCreateIn.title: APIKeyCreateIn

APIKeyCreateOut.description:
Response from POST /v1/api-keys.

``raw_key`` is the only place the plaintext is ever surfaced. The client
is responsible for storing it (e.g. as a CI secret); a subsequent GET on
the key returns the metadata-only :class:`APIKeyListItem` shape.

raw_key.description: The plaintext bearer key (format: tos_<prefix>_<secret>). Returned exactly once at issuance; capture it client-side. Subsequent reads only return metadata.
raw_key.title: Raw Key

APIKeyCreateOut.title: APIKeyCreateOut

APIKeyListItem.description: List-row shape — never includes the plaintext or the hash.
APIKeyListItem.title: APIKeyListItem

AdminDiskItem.description:
One mount / volume entry returned by ``GET /v1/admin/disk``.

``used_pct`` is computed server-side as ``used_bytes / total_bytes * 100``
rounded to one decimal so the UI does not need to repeat the math.
Threshold cells are static configuration (80% warn, 90% critical) for
Phase 4; future PRs may make them per-mount tunable.

error.description: Set when telemetry could not be collected (e.g. mount missing).
error.title: Error

path.description: Filesystem path the bytes were read from (only set for ``workspace`` / ``dt_volume`` — DB-backed entries have no single canonical path).
path.title: Path

status.description: ok / degraded / down derived from ``used_pct`` against the thresholds. ``degraded`` = warning band, ``down`` = critical.
status.title: Status

AdminDiskItem.title: AdminDiskItem

AdminScanListItem.description: One row in the admin scan queue listing — joins scan + project + team.

finished_at.description: Mapped from ``scans.completed_at``. Renamed for clarity in the admin queue UI.
finished_at.title: Finished At

AdminScanListItem.title: AdminScanListItem

AdminTeamDetail.description: Full detail view used by the right-side drawer in the Teams admin.
AdminTeamDetail.title: AdminTeamDetail

AdminTeamListItem.description: Row in the paginated team list — includes counts for the admin table.
AdminTeamListItem.title: AdminTeamListItem

AdminTeamMember.description: Embedded member row in the team detail response.
AdminTeamMember.title: AdminTeamMember

AdminUserDetail.description: Full detail view used by the right-side drawer in the Users admin.
AdminUserDetail.title: AdminUserDetail

AdminUserListItem.description: Row in the paginated list response (lightweight).
AdminUserListItem.title: AdminUserListItem

team_id.description: Required when role is team_admin or developer; ignored for super_admin.
team_id.title: Team Id

AffectedComponent.description: A component_version affected by the same CVE in the same scan.

fixed_version.description: Version that remediates this CVE for this component, when the scan pipeline could determine one from DT findings (v2.2). Per-(component × CVE): the same CVE may be fixed at different versions across packages. ``null`` when DT reported no fix version, or for findings scanned before v2.2.
fixed_version.title: Fixed Version

AffectedComponent.title: AffectedComponent

AffectedComponentByLicense.description: A component_version that carries the license shown in the drawer.

source_path.description: Repo-relative path of the file that produced the finding (e.g. LICENSE, package.json). Null when ORT did not record one.
source_path.title: Source Path

AffectedComponentByLicense.title: AffectedComponentByLicense

AffectedComponentByObligation.description:
A component_version in the latest scan that carries the parent license.

Mirrors :class:`schemas.license_detail.AffectedComponentByLicense` but
keyed via the obligation's parent license rather than a license_finding
row directly.
AffectedComponentByObligation.title: AffectedComponentByObligation

ApprovalCreateIn.description: Request body for creating a new approval request.
ApprovalCreateIn.title: ApprovalCreateIn

ApprovalOut.description: Single component-approval row, safe to serialise to the caller.
ApprovalOut.title: ApprovalOut

ApprovalStatus.description:
Python mirror of the ``approval_status`` Postgres ENUM.

Using ``str`` as the mixin lets Pydantic / JSON serialisation treat the
value as a plain string without extra coercion.
ApprovalStatus.title: ApprovalStatus

ApprovalTransitionIn.description:
Request body for transitioning an approval's status.

``action`` is limited to the three states a human reviewer can drive.
The server assigns ``pending`` on create; it is never a valid action here.
``decision_note`` is optional for ``under_review`` but strongly encouraged
for ``rejected`` (the UI should warn, not block).
ApprovalTransitionIn.title: ApprovalTransitionIn

AuditLogItem.description: One row in the admin audit log search response.

actor_email.description: Joined from ``users.email`` for display. ``null`` when the actor was deleted (FK is ``ondelete='SET NULL'``) or the row was system-initiated.
actor_email.title: Actor Email

AuditLogItem.title: AuditLogItem

BackupInfo.description: One row in the admin backup listing.

db_revision.description: Alembic head recorded in the manifest at backup time. ``None`` when the manifest was missing or unreadable (rare — emitted only by the script's ``unknown`` fallback).
db_revision.title: Db Revision

name.description: Backup directory name. Format: ``{kind}-YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ``. Used as the path component for download / delete / restore.
name.title: Name

BackupInfo.title: BackupInfo

name.description: Pre-computed backup directory name the task will create. Useful for the UI to poll the listing without waiting for task completion.
name.title: Name

upload.description: An OpenVEX or CycloneDX VEX JSON document (format auto-detected).
upload.title: Upload

upload.description: A .zip archive of the project source tree.
upload.title: Upload

BreakerResetOut.description:
Response of ``POST /v1/admin/dt/breaker/reset``.

Reports the transition observed by the operator-triggered reset so the
UI can render "OPEN → CLOSED" without an extra status round-trip and
the audit log captures the same numbers without re-querying Redis.
BreakerResetOut.title: BreakerResetOut

ComponentDetailResponse.description: Drawer payload for a single component in a project's latest scan.

depth.description: Shortest-path distance from a dependency-graph root (v2.2): 1 = direct, 2+ = transitive. ``null`` when the scan carried no dependency graph.
depth.title: Depth

direct.description: True when this is a direct dependency (graph depth 1).
direct.title: Direct

ComponentDetailResponse.title: ComponentDetailResponse

ComponentListResponse.description: Page of components for a project, derived from its latest scan.
ComponentListResponse.title: ComponentListResponse

ComponentSummary.description: One row in the components tab list. Optimized for table rendering.

depth.description: Shortest-path distance from a dependency-graph root (v2.2): 1 = direct, 2+ = transitive. The shallowest path when the component appears at several. ``null`` when the scan carried no dependency graph (older scans / flat-list ecosystems).
depth.title: Depth

direct.description: True when this component is a direct dependency of the scanned project (graph depth 1). False for transitive deps and when the graph was unavailable.
direct.title: Direct

license.description: SPDX id of the worst-category license, or its name if no SPDX id.
license.title: License

ComponentSummary.title: ComponentSummary

DTOrphanItem.description: One orphan candidate detected by the inline scan against DT projects.
DTOrphanItem.title: DTOrphanItem

version.description: DT version string when reachable; null when the breaker is OPEN.
version.title: Version

DependencyChangeOut.description: One applied range edit in the proposed manifest.

after.description: The range string after the edit.
after.title: After

before.description: The range string before the edit.
before.title: Before

package.description: The npm package name (scoped names kept).
package.title: Package

section.description: Manifest block the entry lives in (dependencies / devDependencies / optionalDependencies / peerDependencies).
section.title: Section

DependencyChangeOut.title: DependencyChangeOut

DryRunRecommendationOut.description: One npm component the dry-run proposes to bump (advisory).

current_version.description: The version the latest scan saw.
current_version.title: Current Version

package.description: The npm package name (scoped names kept).
package.title: Package

recommended_version.description: The minimum-safe upgrade target (from the a3 engine).
recommended_version.title: Recommended Version

DryRunRecommendationOut.title: DryRunRecommendationOut

GateResultResponse.description: Build-gate verdict for the project's latest successful scan.

critical_cve_count.description: Number of open critical-severity findings on the evaluated scan. Open = status not in (not_affected, fixed, false_positive).
critical_cve_count.title: Critical Cve Count

epss_gate_count.description: Number of open findings on the evaluated scan whose CVE has an EPSS score at or above ``epss_threshold``. Always 0 when the EPSS gate is disabled (``epss_threshold == null``).
epss_gate_count.title: Epss Gate Count

epss_threshold.description: The active EPSS gate threshold in [0, 1], read from the ``GATE_EPSS_THRESHOLD`` environment variable at evaluation time. ``null`` when the EPSS gate is disabled (unset/unparseable env), in which case the gate behaves exactly as the critical-CVE + forbidden-license gate.
epss_threshold.title: Epss Threshold

evaluated_at.description: Server timestamp at which the verdict was computed (UTC, ISO-8601).
evaluated_at.title: Evaluated At

forbidden_license_count.description: Distinct component_versions on the evaluated scan that carry at least one forbidden-classification license.
forbidden_license_count.title: Forbidden License Count

gate.description: Overall outcome. ``pass`` when no critical CVEs and no forbidden licenses are present, otherwise ``fail``.
gate.title: Gate

reachable_critical_cve_count.description: Subset of the open critical findings on the evaluated scan that an analyser has additionally proven REACHABLE (reachable IS TRUE) — a v2.3 priority signal. Always populated; ``0`` when no finding is proven reachable or no reachability analysis has run. By default this does NOT change the verdict (it is informational), unless the reachable-only critical mode is enabled (see ``reachable_gate_enforced``).
reachable_critical_cve_count.title: Reachable Critical Cve Count

reachable_gate_enforced.description: Whether the opt-in reachable-only critical mode (``GATE_REACHABLE_CRITICAL_ONLY`` env) was active for this evaluation. When ``false`` (default) every open critical counts — the legacy behaviour. When ``true`` the relaxation is requested, but it is applied SAFELY: it takes effect only on scans actually reachability-analysed (reachability is Go-only today), and even then it excludes ONLY criticals PROVEN unreachable — criticals not yet analysed (reachable IS NULL) keep blocking. On a scan with no reachability analysis the gate falls back to counting every open critical, so the flag never silently disables the gate for un-analysed ecosystems.
reachable_gate_enforced.title: Reachable Gate Enforced

reason.description: Human-readable explanation when ``gate == 'fail'``. ``null`` for passing builds.
reason.title: Reason

scan_id.description: ID of the scan the verdict was computed against. ``null`` when the project has never had a successful scan, in which case ``gate == 'pass'`` is returned by convention (no signal = no block).
scan_id.title: Scan Id

GateResultResponse.title: GateResultResponse

GitHubAppCredentialCreateIn.description:
Request body for registering a GitHub App credential.

``private_key`` is the plaintext PEM — the ONLY place it is accepted. It is
encrypted before persist and never returned.

private_key.description: The GitHub App PEM private key (plaintext). Accepted ONCE at registration, encrypted at rest, and never returned.
private_key.title: Private Key

GitHubAppCredentialCreateIn.title: GitHubAppCredentialCreateIn

GitHubAppCredentialListPage.description: Paginated list of GitHub App credentials.
GitHubAppCredentialListPage.title: GitHubAppCredentialListPage

GitHubAppCredentialOut.description:
Response shape for a credential — metadata only, NEVER any key material.

``has_private_key`` is always True for a persisted credential (the column is
NOT NULL); it is surfaced explicitly so the UI can render a "configured"
state without the schema ever carrying the key or its ciphertext.
GitHubAppCredentialOut.title: GitHubAppCredentialOut

GitHubAppInstallationLinkIn.description: Request body for linking (opting-in) an installation to a project.

project_id.description: The TrustedOSS project this installation is opted-in to.
project_id.title: Project Id

GitHubAppInstallationLinkIn.title: GitHubAppInstallationLinkIn

GitHubAppInstallationListPage.description: Paginated list of installations under a credential.
GitHubAppInstallationListPage.title: GitHubAppInstallationListPage

GitHubAppInstallationOut.description: Installation row — no secrets at all.
GitHubAppInstallationOut.title: GitHubAppInstallationOut

HealthComponent.description: One probe in the system-health summary.

value.description: Numeric reading (e.g. ``celery`` worker count, ``active_scans`` row count, ``last_24h_errors`` row count). ``null`` for boolean probes.
value.title: Value

HealthComponent.title: HealthComponent

LicenseDetailResponse.description: Full drawer payload for a single license_findings row.

affected_components.description: All component_versions in the same scan that carry this license, across every kind (declared / concluded / detected). Capped at 500 rows — see ``affected_components_truncated``.
affected_components.title: Affected Components

affected_components_total.description: Total number of distinct component_versions associated with this license in the scan, before the response cap is applied.
affected_components_total.title: Affected Components Total

affected_components_truncated.description: True when the server truncated ``affected_components`` to its 500-row cap. Clients should display a notice and optionally fall back to the components tab for the full list.
affected_components_truncated.title: Affected Components Truncated

finding_kind.description: ORT classification kind on this specific finding row.
finding_kind.title: Finding Kind

id.description: license_findings.id (the row the URL points at).
id.title: Id

ort_match.description: Best-effort pass-through of license_findings.raw_data. The shape is not contractual: ORT may include matched-text excerpts, license-detector confidence, copyright statements, and similar. Frontends should render this defensively. ``null`` when the scan pipeline did not emit any raw data for the finding.
ort_match.title: Ort Match

LicenseDetailResponse.title: LicenseDetailResponse

LicenseDistribution.description:
Counts of distinct component_versions per category in the latest scan.

Always returns all four buckets (zero if absent) so the bar chart renders
a stable axis. This is the single source of truth shared with the Overview
tab's ``license_distribution`` — no duplicate aggregator endpoint.
LicenseDistribution.title: LicenseDistribution

LicenseException.description:
One explicit allow-regardless-of-category waiver.

``spdx_id`` + ``reason`` are required. ``expires_at`` (optional) lets c2
treat an expired waiver as absent. ``component_purl`` (optional) scopes the
waiver to a single component; absent → applies to any component carrying
``spdx_id``. Extra keys are rejected so a typo cannot silently smuggle an
un-validated field through the JSONB column.
LicenseException.title: LicenseException

LicenseListItem.description:
One row in the Licenses tab table.

A row represents a single license observed in the project's latest scan,
aggregated across all component_versions that carry it. ``id`` carries the
license_findings row id of a representative finding (usually the
lexicographically first by source_path) so the drawer endpoint has a
stable handle to dereference.

affected_count.description: Distinct component_versions in the latest scan that carry this license.
affected_count.title: Affected Count

id.description: license_findings.id of a representative finding for this license in the latest scan. Used as the drawer's primary key.
id.title: Id

kind.description: ORT classification kind on the representative finding (declared / concluded / detected).
kind.title: Kind

sample_finding_id.description: Finding row to pass to GET /v1/license_findings/{id} when the user opens the drawer. Echoes ``id`` today; a separate field is kept so frontends can stay forward-compatible if the list shape ever needs to advertise multiple sample findings.
sample_finding_id.title: Sample Finding Id

spdx_id.description: SPDX short identifier (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only, ...). Null for ORT custom licenses (LicenseRef-*).
spdx_id.title: Spdx Id

LicenseListItem.title: LicenseListItem

LicenseListResponse.description: Page of licenses for the project's latest scan.
LicenseListResponse.title: LicenseListResponse

LicenseMatch.description: A per-line license match projected from the folded scancode JSON.

score.description: scancode match score (0-100), or null when unreported.
score.title: Score

spdx_id.description: SPDX identifier of the matched license.
spdx_id.title: Spdx Id

LicenseMatch.title: LicenseMatch

LicensePolicyOut.description: ORM-derived response shape for a single license policy.
LicensePolicyOut.title: LicensePolicyOut

LicensePolicyUpsertIn.description:
Request body for PUT (upsert) of a team or org license policy.

All fields are optional with sensible defaults so a minimal ``{}`` body
creates an empty (effectively no-op) policy. Strict validation rejects
oversized / malformed maps with a 422 (never a 500).
LicensePolicyUpsertIn.title: LicensePolicyUpsertIn

NotificationListResponse.description:
Paginated list of notifications + the bell badge count.

``unread_count`` is the global unread count for the caller (NOT the
unread count within this page) — the SPA renders it as a badge in the
nav, independent of the drawer's pagination state.
NotificationListResponse.title: NotificationListResponse

NotificationOut.description:
One row in the notifications list response.

Frozen contract — the SPA's notification drawer depends on every field
name and shape. Add new optional fields as nullable; do not rename.
NotificationOut.title: NotificationOut

NotificationPrefsIn.description:
Per-user notification channel toggles (PUT body — full row).

The endpoint is PUT, not PATCH: every channel must be supplied. Sending
a partial body would be a 422 (Pydantic enforces required fields).
NotificationPrefsIn.title: NotificationPrefsIn

NpmDryRunRequest.description:
Optional uploaded ``package.json`` body.

When omitted, the endpoint best-effort fetches the manifest from the
project's latest preserved scan source. Supplying ``manifest`` is the
reliable path when the source was never preserved (or was swept).

manifest.description: Raw package.json text to edit. When omitted, the endpoint reads the manifest from the latest preserved scan source (best-effort).
manifest.title: Manifest

NpmDryRunRequest.title: NpmDryRunRequest

changed.description: True iff at least one dependency range was rewritten.
changed.title: Changed

edited_manifest.description: The proposed edited package.json text (only present when changed); the lockfile is NOT edited — regenerate it with `npm install`.
edited_manifest.title: Edited Manifest

manifest_found.description: True iff a manifest was available to edit.
manifest_found.title: Manifest Found

manifest_source.description: Where the manifest came from: 'override' (request body), 'preserved_source' (latest scan tarball), or 'none' (not available).
manifest_source.title: Manifest Source

notes.description: High-level notes about the dry-run (e.g. 'no manifest found').
notes.title: Notes

project_id.description: The project the dry-run is for.
project_id.title: Project Id

recommendations.description: The npm upgrade recommendations considered for the edit.
recommendations.title: Recommendations

scan_id.description: The scan the recommendations were derived from (latest scan).
scan_id.title: Scan Id

NpmPullRequestCreate.description:
Optional uploaded ``package.json`` body (mirrors the b2 dry-run request).

When omitted, the service best-effort fetches the manifest from the project's
latest preserved scan source. NOTE: the target repository is NOT part of this
body — it is derived from the project's opted-in GitHub App installation, so a
caller can never point the PR at an arbitrary repo.

manifest.description: Raw package.json text to edit. When omitted, the service reads the manifest from the latest preserved scan source (best-effort).
manifest.title: Manifest

NpmPullRequestCreate.title: NpmPullRequestCreate

OAuthIdentityListResponse.description:
Response wrapper for ``GET /v1/users/me/oauth-identities``.

Sorted oldest-first; the caller renders the list as "first connected
on T". The response has no pagination — a single user is unlikely to
accumulate enough identities to need it (GitHub + Google + maybe a
future SSO IdP).
OAuthIdentityListResponse.title: OAuthIdentityListResponse

OAuthIdentityOut.description:
One linked OAuth identity in the self-service list response.

Frozen contract — every field name + shape is depended on by the SPA
profile page. Add new fields as nullable; never rename.
OAuthIdentityOut.title: OAuthIdentityOut

ObligationDetailResponse.description: Full drawer payload for a single obligation, scoped to a project.

affected_components.description: All component_versions in the project's latest scan that carry the parent license. Capped at 500 rows — see ``affected_components_truncated``.
affected_components.title: Affected Components

affected_components_total.description: Total number of distinct component_versions associated with the parent license in the scan, before the response cap is applied.
affected_components_total.title: Affected Components Total

affected_components_truncated.description: True when the server truncated ``affected_components`` to its 500-row cap. Clients should display a notice and optionally fall back to the components tab for the full list.
affected_components_truncated.title: Affected Components Truncated

link.description: Optional URL provided by the catalog. Frontends MUST scheme-filter to http/https before rendering as a clickable link.
link.title: Link

text.description: Human-readable obligation text. Capped at 65 536 bytes — see ``text_truncated``.
text.title: Text

text_truncated.description: True when the server truncated ``text`` to its 65 536-byte cap. Clients should display a notice and offer a link to the source catalog if available.
text_truncated.title: Text Truncated

ObligationDetailResponse.title: ObligationDetailResponse

ObligationListItem.description:
One row in the Obligations tab table.

A row is a single ``(license, obligation_kind)`` pair observed in the
project's latest scan. ``affected_count`` counts distinct
component_versions that carry the parent license — not the obligation
itself, which is a per-license policy attribute. The drawer dereferences
by ``id`` (the obligation row) within the project scope.

affected_count.description: Distinct component_versions in the latest scan that carry the parent license.
affected_count.title: Affected Count

kind.description: Obligation kind — free-form catalog string (e.g. attribution, source-disclosure, copyleft). See KNOWN_OBLIGATION_KINDS for the ranked allow-list rendered first in the distribution.
kind.title: Kind

license_name.description: Full name of the parent license.
license_name.title: License Name

license_spdx_id.description: SPDX short identifier of the parent license. Null for ORT custom licenses (LicenseRef-*).
license_spdx_id.title: License Spdx Id

link.description: Optional URL with further explanation of the obligation. Frontends MUST scheme-filter this before rendering as a link.
link.title: Link

ObligationListItem.title: ObligationListItem

ObligationListResponse.description:
Page of obligations for the project's latest scan.

``distribution`` is the per-kind count across all obligations visible in
the latest scan, regardless of the active filter. Known kinds appear
first in their canonical order; unknown kinds (catalog growth) are
appended alphabetically. Always populated even on empty result sets.

distribution.description: kind → count of distinct (license, kind) obligation rows in the latest scan. Known kinds appear first in canonical order, followed by unknown kinds alphabetically.
distribution.title: Distribution

ObligationListResponse.title: ObligationListResponse

count.description: Number of UUIDs the task will attempt to delete. Zero means the task will scan the full catalog and delete every orphan it discovers.
count.title: Count

OrphanCleanupRequest.description:
Body of ``POST /v1/admin/dt/orphans/cleanup``.

When ``dt_project_uuids`` is empty / omitted, the Celery task scans the
full DT project catalog and deletes every orphan it finds. When the
field is populated the task only processes the supplied UUIDs, which
is the path the admin UI uses for "select rows + cleanup".
OrphanCleanupRequest.title: OrphanCleanupRequest

dry_run.description: When ``true`` the endpoint builds the Markdown comment but does not call GitHub. Useful for local CI rehearsals and used by the default integration tests so they do not require network access.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

repo_full_name.description: GitHub ``owner/repo`` slug. Validated against the GitHub naming rules so we never call api.github.com with attacker-controlled path segments.
repo_full_name.title: Repo Full Name

body_preview.description: The first 280 characters of the rendered comment body. The full body is never returned because it can grow large; the preview is enough for the CI runner to log a sanity check.
body_preview.title: Body Preview

comment_id.description: GitHub issue-comment id. ``null`` for ``dry_run`` and on transport errors that we choose not to surface to the caller.
comment_id.title: Comment Id

gate.description: Echo of the gate verdict the comment reports. Lets the CI runner branch on the build-blocking decision in a single round-trip.
gate.title: Gate

ProjectListResponse.description: Page of projects + total count for client-side paging UI.
ProjectListResponse.title: ProjectListResponse

ProjectOverviewResponse.description: Aggregated risk / scan picture for the project detail Overview tab.

current_user_role.description: The requesting user's effective role *within this project's owning team*: 'super_admin' for platform superusers, otherwise the user's membership role on the project's team ('team_admin' / 'developer'). Users who can read the project via org-wide visibility but hold no membership default to the least-privileged 'developer'. The frontend uses this (not the global JWT role) to gate team-scoped actions such as vulnerability suppression (BUG-005).
current_user_role.title: Current User Role

license_distribution.description: Count of components per license category. Keys are a subset of {forbidden, conditional, allowed, unknown}.
license_distribution.title: License Distribution

risk_score.description: Composite risk 0–100 derived from severity + license distribution. Formula: min(100, critical*15 + high*5 + medium*1 + forbidden*30 + conditional*5).
risk_score.title: Risk Score

severity_distribution.description: Count of components per severity bucket. Keys are a subset of {critical, high, medium, low, info, none}. Buckets with zero components are still included so frontends can render an empty bar.
severity_distribution.title: Severity Distribution

ProjectOverviewResponse.title: ProjectOverviewResponse

ProjectPublic.description: Outbound shape for every project-bearing response.
ProjectPublic.title: ProjectPublic

ProjectUpdate.description:
Inbound payload for PATCH /v1/projects/{project_id}.

`team_id` and `slug` are intentionally NOT updatable: changing the team
would require re-scoping every audit log, scan, and finding; changing the
slug would invalidate webhook URLs and CLI bookmarks. If the product ever
needs slug rename, model it as a separate `POST /v1/projects/{id}:rename`
operation that does the rewrite in one transaction.
ProjectUpdate.title: ProjectUpdate

RemediationPackageChangeOut.description:
One package bump recorded on the PR (for audit / human review).

``populate_by_name`` lets the service build this from keyword args
(``from_version`` / ``to_version``) while the wire / JSONB shape uses the
natural ``from`` / ``to`` keys via the field aliases (``from`` is a Python
keyword, hence the suffix on the attribute name).

from.description: The version the scan saw (advisory; may be null).
from.title: From

package.description: The npm package name (scoped names kept).
package.title: Package

to.description: The minimum-safe upgrade target the PR applies.
to.title: To

RemediationPackageChangeOut.title: RemediationPackageChangeOut

RemediationPullRequestList.description: A page of remediation-PR records for a project (newest first).
RemediationPullRequestList.title: RemediationPullRequestList

RemediationPullRequestOut.description: The persisted remediation-PR record returned to the UI.

base_branch.description: The repo default branch the PR targets.
base_branch.title: Base Branch

head_branch.description: The branch the portal created for the bump.
head_branch.title: Head Branch

package_changes.description: The package bumps the PR applies.
package_changes.title: Package Changes

pr_number.description: The GitHub PR number (null until opened).
pr_number.title: Pr Number

pr_url.description: The GitHub PR URL (null until opened).
pr_url.title: Pr Url

repository_full_name.description: The 'owner/repo' the PR targets (derived from the opt-in link).
repository_full_name.title: Repository Full Name

updated_at.description: When the record was last updated.
updated_at.title: Updated At

RemediationPullRequestOut.title: RemediationPullRequestOut

RemediationWarningOut.description: A non-fatal note about the dry-run (skip reason / lockfile guidance).

examples.detail: package.json was edited; run `npm install` to regenerate package-lock.json

package.description: The package the warning concerns, if any.
package.title: Package

RemediationWarningOut.title: RemediationWarningOut

ResetPasswordRequest.description:
Inbound payload for POST /auth/reset-password.

The new password reuses the registration policy (≥ 12 chars / NIST
800-63B baseline). The token is a URL-safe string up to ~64 chars
(``secrets.token_urlsafe(32)`` produces ~43 chars; we cap at 256 for
defence in depth against pathological inputs).
ResetPasswordRequest.title: ResetPasswordRequest

ScanCreate.description:
Inbound payload for POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/scans.

`kind` selects the scan pipeline (source = cdxgen + ORT + DT;
container = Trivy). All scan inputs (git_ref, image_ref, ORT options)
travel inside `metadata` so the schema does not have to grow a field
every time the pipeline learns a new knob.
ScanCreate.title: ScanCreate

ScanListResponse.description: Page of scans for a project.
ScanListResponse.title: ScanListResponse

ScanPublic.description: Outbound shape for every scan-bearing response.
ScanPublic.title: ScanPublic

ScanSummary.description: Compact scan record used by the project overview's recent-scans list.
ScanSummary.title: ScanSummary

SourceArchiveUploadResponse.description:
Outbound shape for POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/source-archive.

The opaque ``archive_id`` is later echoed into ``ScanCreate.metadata`` as
``{"source_type": "upload", "archive_id": "<id>"}`` to scan the uploaded
source instead of cloning a git URL.
SourceArchiveUploadResponse.title: SourceArchiveUploadResponse

SourceFileResponse.description: A single source file's bytes (capped) + its per-line license matches.

byte_size.description: Full uncompressed size of the file in bytes.
byte_size.title: Byte Size

content.description: Decoded file content (possibly truncated). Null for binary files.
content.title: Content

encoding.description: 'utf-8' when ``content`` is decoded text; 'binary' when the file is non-text (NUL byte or undecodable) and ``content`` is null.
encoding.title: Encoding

license_matches.description: Per-line license matches for THIS path, projected from the folded scancode JSON. Empty when the file has no recorded matches.
license_matches.title: License Matches

path.description: POSIX path of the file relative to the source root.
path.title: Path

scan_id.description: The scan whose preserved source this file was read from.
scan_id.title: Scan Id

truncated.description: True when ``content`` was capped at the viewer's per-file byte limit and does not contain the whole file.
truncated.title: Truncated

SourceFileResponse.title: SourceFileResponse

SourceTreeEntry.description: One immediate child (file or directory) of a listed directory.

byte_size.description: Uncompressed size of the file in bytes. 0 for directories (their size is not meaningful in the tar).
byte_size.title: Byte Size

is_dir.description: True for a directory, False for a regular file.
is_dir.title: Is Dir

license_spdx_ids.description: Cheap per-file license badge set: the distinct SPDX ids recorded in license_findings for this exact source path under the resolved scan. Empty for directories and unanalysed files.
license_spdx_ids.title: License Spdx Ids

name.description: Base name of the entry (no path separators).
name.title: Name

path.description: POSIX path of the entry relative to the source root.
path.title: Path

SourceTreeEntry.title: SourceTreeEntry

SourceTreePage.description: A page of immediate children for one directory.

entries.description: Immediate children of ``path`` on this page (dirs first).
entries.title: Entries

path.description: The directory whose children are listed. Empty string is the source root.
path.title: Path

scan_id.description: The scan whose preserved source this tree was read from.
scan_id.title: Scan Id

size.description: Page size used for this response.
size.title: Size

total.description: Total number of immediate children in ``path`` (all pages).
total.title: Total

SourceTreePage.title: SourceTreePage

TeamMembershipPublic.description: Team-membership info embedded in admin user/team responses.
TeamMembershipPublic.title: TeamMembershipPublic

UnreadCountOut.description: Minimal payload for the bell badge poll.
UnreadCountOut.title: UnreadCountOut

UpgradeRecommendation.description:
Minimum-safe-upgrade recommendation for a finding's component (v2.2 2.2-a3).

Computed from the per-finding ``fixed_version`` values (2.2-a1) of *all* the
component's open CVEs in the same scan: ``recommended_version`` is their
semver maximum — the lowest version that resolves every open finding for
this component at once. ``null`` ``recommended_version`` with a ``reason``
means we declined to recommend (see below); the UI then shows a "no
recommendation" hint rather than a misleading partial upgrade.

The ``direct`` / ``max_severity`` / ``max_epss`` fields are *priority
signals* (not used to compute the version) so the drawer / PR comment can
flag the highest-leverage upgrades — a direct dependency with a high-EPSS
critical CVE is the one to bump first.

direct.description: Priority signal: the component is a direct dependency (the scan's ``direct`` flag or graph depth == 1) — actionable in the developer's own manifest right now.
direct.title: Direct

finding_count.description: Number of the component's open findings the recommendation reflects.
finding_count.title: Finding Count

max_epss.description: Priority signal: highest EPSS exploit-probability among the open findings.
max_epss.title: Max Epss

max_severity.description: Priority signal: highest CVE severity among the component's open findings.
max_severity.title: Max Severity

reason.description: Why ``recommended_version`` is set or null: 'ok' (computed); 'no_fix_version' (an open finding has no known fix, so a partial upgrade would be misleading); 'unparseable_version' (all fix strings were malformed); 'no_open_findings' (the component is fully dispositioned).
reason.title: Reason

recommended_version.description: Semver maximum of the component's open findings' fix versions — the lowest version that resolves all of this component's open CVEs in the scan. ``null`` when no recommendation could be made (see ``reason``).
recommended_version.title: Recommended Version

UpgradeRecommendation.title: UpgradeRecommendation

UserPublic.description: Shape returned for every user-bearing response. Never includes secrets.
UserPublic.title: UserPublic

VEXImportItemError.description:
One structured skip/error row in the import summary.

Identifies the offending statement by its VEX coordinates (vulnerability id
+ product/affects ref) so the analyst can locate it in the source document,
plus a closed-vocabulary ``reason`` and a human-readable ``detail``.

reason.description: Closed-vocabulary reason the statement was not applied.
reason.title: Reason

vulnerability.description: CVE/GHSA/OSV id the statement targeted (None if absent).
vulnerability.title: Vulnerability

VEXImportItemError.title: VEXImportItemError

VEXImportSummary.description:
Result panel for a VEX import.

Counts are computed over *findings*, not raw statements: a statement that
matches three component versions contributes three to ``matched`` and up to
three to ``applied``. ``skipped`` + ``applied`` over the matched set, plus
any unmatched statements, are reflected as ``errors`` rows.

applied.description: Findings whose status was actually changed.
applied.title: Applied

format.description: Auto-detected source format of the uploaded document.
format.title: Format

matched.description: Findings that a statement resolved to (before transition).
matched.title: Matched

skipped.description: Findings/statements deliberately not applied (no-op, illegal transition, unknown vuln/purl, ambiguous match, …).
skipped.title: Skipped

VEXImportSummary.title: VEXImportSummary

VexOrigin.description:
Provenance of the VEX document that drove a finding's last transition.

Populated only when ``analysis_source == 'vex_import'`` (NULL otherwise).
Shape mirrors what ``services/vex_import.py`` persists onto the
``vex_origin`` JSONB column: the document @id / serialNumber, author,
document timestamp, the VEX status the matching statement carried, and when
the import ran. All fields are optional because the two VEX dialects carry
different provenance and producers routinely omit fields.

Security note: every field here is analyst-/document-supplied text. The
frontend renders it via React's default text escaping (never
``dangerouslySetInnerHTML``); the storage layer already strips control
chars / NULs. ``extra="allow"`` keeps any future provenance key the import
persists, but the UI only renders the typed fields.

author.description: Document author (OpenVEX author / CycloneDX first tool).
author.title: Author

format.description: Source VEX dialect of the consuming document.
format.title: Format

id.description: OpenVEX @id or CycloneDX serialNumber of the document.
id.title: Id

timestamp.description: Document timestamp, as carried by the source document.
timestamp.title: Timestamp

vex_status.description: The raw VEX status the matching statement carried.
vex_status.title: Vex Status

VexOrigin.title: VexOrigin

VulnerabilityDetailResponse.description: Full drawer payload for a single vulnerability_findings row.

analysis_source.description: Provenance of the current status: 'vex_import' when an uploaded VEX document auto-transitioned this finding, 'manual' (or NULL on legacy rows) otherwise. Drives the drawer's provenance badge.
analysis_source.title: Analysis Source

epss_percentile.description: EPSS percentile rank for the CVE, in [0, 1]. ``null`` when no EPSS publication exists. Serialized from Numeric(6,5) as a float.
epss_percentile.title: Epss Percentile

epss_score.description: EPSS exploit-probability for the CVE, in [0, 1]. ``null`` when no EPSS publication exists. Serialized from Numeric(6,5) as a float.
epss_score.title: Epss Score

reachability_analyzed_at.description: When the reachability signal was last written (UTC, ISO-8601). ``null`` until a reachability run touches this finding.
reachability_analyzed_at.title: Reachability Analyzed At

reachability_source.description: Identifier of the analyser that produced ``reachable`` ('govulncheck' today). ``null`` when ``reachable`` is ``null``.
reachability_source.title: Reachability Source

reachable.description: Tri-state reachability signal (v2.3). ``true`` = the vulnerable symbol is reachable on the project's call graph; ``false`` = analysed and NOT reachable; ``null`` = not analysed. Drives the drawer's reachability badge.
reachable.title: Reachable

upgrade_recommendation.description: Minimum-safe-upgrade recommendation for this finding's component (v2.2 2.2-a3). ``null`` only on legacy responses; otherwise present with a ``reason`` even when no concrete version could be recommended.

vex_origin.description: Provenance of the consuming VEX document when analysis_source == 'vex_import' (NULL otherwise).

VulnerabilityDetailResponse.title: VulnerabilityDetailResponse

VulnerabilityListItem.description: One row in the Vulnerabilities tab table.

affected_component_count.description: Distinct component_versions in the same scan affected by this CVE. 1 in the common case; >1 only when several distinct packages bundle the same vulnerable code.
affected_component_count.title: Affected Component Count

analysis_source.description: Provenance of the row's current status: 'vex_import' when an uploaded VEX document auto-transitioned it, 'manual' (or NULL on legacy rows) otherwise. Lets the list render a 'VEX' provenance marker and back the 'suppressed via VEX' filter.
analysis_source.title: Analysis Source

discovered_at.description: When this finding row was first inserted (vulnerability_findings.created_at).
discovered_at.title: Discovered At

epss_percentile.description: EPSS percentile rank for the CVE, in [0, 1]. ``null`` when no EPSS publication exists. Serialized from Numeric(6,5) as a float.
epss_percentile.title: Epss Percentile

epss_score.description: EPSS exploit-probability for the CVE, in [0, 1]. ``null`` when the upstream feed has not published an EPSS score for this CVE. Serialized from the Numeric(6,5) column as a float.
epss_score.title: Epss Score

reachability_analyzed_at.description: When the reachability signal was last written (UTC, ISO-8601). ``null`` until a reachability run touches this finding — lets the UI distinguish a stale signal from a never-analysed one.
reachability_analyzed_at.title: Reachability Analyzed At

reachability_source.description: Identifier of the analyser that produced ``reachable`` ('govulncheck' today). ``null`` when ``reachable`` is ``null``.
reachability_source.title: Reachability Source

reachable.description: Tri-state reachability signal (v2.3). ``true`` = the vulnerable symbol is reachable on the project's call graph; ``false`` = an analyser ran and concluded it is NOT reachable; ``null`` = not analysed (no reachability run, or the package was out of the analyser's language scope). Drives the list's reachability badge and the ``?reachable=`` filter / ``sort=reachable`` ranking.
reachable.title: Reachable

VulnerabilityListItem.title: VulnerabilityListItem

VulnerabilityListResponse.description: Page of CVE findings for a project's latest scan.
VulnerabilityListResponse.title: VulnerabilityListResponse

VulnerabilityRef.description: Compact CVE reference attached to a component detail.

epss_percentile.description: EPSS percentile rank for the CVE, in [0, 1]. ``null`` when unset.
epss_percentile.title: Epss Percentile

epss_score.description: EPSS exploit-probability for the CVE, in [0, 1]. ``null`` when no EPSS publication exists for this CVE.
epss_score.title: Epss Score

fixed_version.description: Version that remediates this CVE for this component, when the scan pipeline could determine one from DT findings (v2.2). ``null`` when DT reported no fix version, or for findings scanned before v2.2.
fixed_version.title: Fixed Version

VulnerabilityRef.title: VulnerabilityRef

VulnerabilityStatusHistoryEntry.description: One transition in the finding's status history (derived from audit_logs).

previous_status.description: Status the row carried *before* this audit row. Derived by walking audit_logs in chronological order; the first entry's previous_status is null because the row started at the server default ('new').
previous_status.title: Previous Status

VulnerabilityStatusHistoryEntry.title: VulnerabilityStatusHistoryEntry

if_match.description: Optional optimistic-concurrency token. When supplied, the server compares this against the current row's updated_at. Mismatch → 409 Conflict (RFC 7807). When omitted, the update proceeds without lock (best-effort).
if_match.title: If Match

justification.description: Free-form note recorded as analysis_justification. Required by the UI for VEX-significant transitions but enforced softly: the API accepts an empty justification (regulators often re-trigger the transition once with the note attached).
justification.title: Justification

info.description: Open-source self-hosted SCA portal — CVE, license compliance, and SBOM management with EPSS prioritization, VEX consumption, CI build gating, and a Dependency-Track circuit breaker.
info.title: TrustedOSS Portal API

post.description:
Public — no authentication required, but limited per
``PASSWORD_RESET_RATE_LIMIT`` (5/min/IP by default).

Body shape: ``{"email": "<address>"}``. Always returns 204 + empty body
(CWE-204). When the address matches a registered user we additionally
enqueue an email via Celery. When the per-email cooldown is active we
set ``Retry-After`` to the configured cooldown and STILL return 204.
post.summary: Request a password reset link (public, rate limited)

post.description:
Public — no authentication required, but limited to 5 attempts/minute/IP.

On success: 200 + access_token in the body, refresh as HttpOnly cookie.
On bad credentials: 401 problem+json.
post.summary: Login (public, rate limited)

post.description:
Revoke the refresh cookie. Idempotent — always returns 204 even if the
cookie is absent or already revoked.
post.summary: Logout (revoke refresh cookie)

get.description: Authenticated. Returns the same shape as /auth/register.
get.summary: Return the currently authenticated user

get.description:
Public — no authentication required.

302 → provider's authorize URL with a signed state. Returns:
  - 503 + ``oauth_provider_disabled`` Problem Details when the
    provider's client id/secret is not configured.
  - 404 Problem Details when the provider name is unknown (FastAPI's
    Literal[] gate normally catches this; the explicit branch survives
    future enum widening).
  - 403 ``demo_read_only`` Problem Details when the deployment runs in
    read-only live-demo mode (OAuth sign-in is a write; see
    :func:`_demo_read_only_blocked`).
get.summary: Begin OAuth sign-in (public)

get.description:
Public — the provider's redirect lands here after consent.

Success path: 302 → ``redirect_after`` (or configured default) with the
refresh-token HttpOnly cookie attached.

Failure path: 302 → configured failure URL with ``?error=<reason>``. The
provider's own ``?error=access_denied`` (user clicked "Cancel") falls
through here too — we forward a normalised ``error=oauth_denied``.

Read-only demo: if ``DEMO_READ_ONLY`` is enabled we 403 BEFORE any token
exchange or DB write (see :func:`_demo_read_only_blocked`), so the callback
can never create/link a User or Team in the demo.
get.summary: OAuth callback (public)

post.description:
Public — the refresh cookie is the credential.

Successful rotation: 200 + new access_token + new refresh cookie.
Reuse detected (cookie already rotated): 401, entire chain revoked.
post.summary: Rotate refresh token (public; refresh cookie is the credential)

post.description:
Public — no authentication required.

Returns the new user (without password). 422 for validation errors,
409 if the email is already registered.
post.summary: Register a new user (public)

post.description:
Public — the reset token is the credential.

On success: 204 + every refresh token for the user is revoked.
On bad / expired / used token: 422 problem+json.
post.summary: Confirm a password reset using a one-shot token (public)

get.description:
Cheap PURE-LIVENESS probe used by docker-compose / k8s liveness checks.

PUBLIC / unauthenticated (CLAUDE.md rule #12 explicit exception). This proves
only that the uvicorn process is accepting requests — it does NOT touch the
database and says nothing about schema state. For "is the schema migrated and
safe to serve traffic / start workers", use GET /health/ready (api/v1/health.py).

v2.1 Track B (B5): also surfaces ``demo_read_only`` so the SPA can render the
read-only banner and disable write actions without needing a separate build.
The flag is resolved at request time (CLAUDE.md rule #11), so the same image
behaves correctly whether DEMO_READ_ONLY is set or not.
get.summary: Liveness probe — PUBLIC, unauthenticated

get.description:
Return 200 when the DB schema matches the Alembic HEAD, else 503.

PUBLIC: no auth dependency by design (probe endpoint — see module docstring
and CLAUDE.md core rule #12). The check is read-only (a single SELECT on
``alembic_version`` plus an in-image read of the script tree).

200.description: Schema is at the Alembic HEAD revision.

example.detail: database schema is not at the expected Alembic HEAD (expected: 0021_abc; current: 0020_def)
example.title: Service Not Ready

503.description: Schema is not at HEAD, the alembic_version table is missing, or the database is unreachable. RFC 7807 problem+json.

get.summary: Readiness probe (schema at Alembic HEAD) — PUBLIC, unauthenticated
Search audit log (admin) — paginated, filterable
Export audit log to CSV (admin) — streaming, capped at 100k rows
List all backups (admin) — auto + manual, newest first

post.summary: Trigger a manual backup (admin) — enqueues the Celery task
Restore from an uploaded backup archive (admin) — destructive

delete.summary: Delete a manual backup (admin) — auto backups are protected

get.summary: Download a backup archive (admin) — streams a tar.gz of the directory
Disk usage telemetry (admin) — workspace / DT volume / Postgres / Redis

post.description:
Operator escape hatch: when DT has recovered but the breaker is stuck OPEN
(e.g. the cooldown window keeps tripping during a flaky restore), this
endpoint forces it back to CLOSED + clears the failure counter.

Refused with 409 + ``dt_breaker_already_closed`` extension when the
breaker is already CLOSED — the operator should investigate why a reset
looked necessary instead of letting a scripted retry no-op silently.
post.summary: Force the DT circuit breaker back to CLOSED (admin) — last-resort recovery
Force a DT health probe (admin) — drives the breaker state

get.summary: List orphan DT projects (admin) — DT projects with no matching local scan

post.summary: Enqueue an orphan-cleanup Celery task (admin) — actually deletes DT projects

get.summary: DT health snapshot (admin) — breaker state + DT version, cached 30s
System health summary (admin) — postgres / redis / celery / DT / disk
List scans (admin) — cross-team queue with optional status filter

post.summary: Force-cancel a scan (admin) — Celery revoke + status='cancelled'

get.summary: List teams (admin) — paginated, name search

post.summary: Create a team (admin)

delete.summary: Delete a team (admin) — archives projects, refuses on active scans

get.summary: Get one team (admin) — detail with members and project count

post.summary: Add (or update) a team member (admin)

get.summary: List users (admin) — paginated, filterable
Get one user (admin) — detail with memberships + scan count

patch.summary: Deactivate a user (admin) — revokes refresh tokens

post.description:
Issues a one-shot reset token (bcrypt-hashed in storage) and returns 204.

Phase 6 PR #18 will wire the SMTP / Slack delivery channel. Until then
the plaintext token is generated, persisted as a hash, audit-logged via
the listener (which masks the hash to ``***``), and discarded.

-- Account-enumeration semantics (security-reviewer F5) -------------------

This endpoint returns 404 when ``user_id`` does not exist. That IS an
enumeration oracle in isolation, but it is acceptable HERE because the
route is super-admin-gated by ``require_super_admin_or_404`` — any
caller who can reach this code path is already authorised to read the
full user list (``GET /v1/admin/users``), so the 404 leaks no
information they did not already have. The trust boundary is ABOVE this
endpoint, not at it.

The Phase 6 PR #18 PUBLIC password-reset flow ("forgot password") MUST
NOT copy this 404-on-miss pattern. That endpoint is unauthenticated, so
a 404 vs. 204 split there would let an attacker enumerate registered
emails (CWE-204 Observable Response Discrepancy). The public flow
returns a uniform 204 regardless of whether the email exists, with the
actual reset email sent only when a match is found. See
``docs/v2-execution-plan.md`` §3.7 for the Phase 6 contract.
post.summary: Initiate a password reset (admin) — Phase 6 wires email delivery

get.summary: Paginated list of API keys visible to the caller

201.description: Key created. ``raw_key`` is the wire bearer string — capture it; subsequent reads only return metadata.

403.description: Caller may not issue at the requested scope.

404.description: Project not found (when scope='project').

422.description: Scope/team_id/project_id combination is invalid (e.g. scope='team' with no team_id).

503.description: Could not allocate a unique key prefix after retries — transient and retryable.

post.summary: Issue a new API key (plaintext returned ONCE in raw_key)

204.description: Key revoked (or was already revoked — idempotent).

403.description: Caller can see the key but lacks permission to revoke it (needs to be the issuer, a team_admin of the key's team, or super_admin).

404.description: Key not found, or not visible to the caller.

get.summary: Paginated list of approval requests (team-scoped; super_admin sees all)

404.description: Project or component not found, or caller lacks team access.

409.description: An open approval already exists for this component + project. ``approval_already_open = true`` extension in the Problem Details body.

post.summary: Open a new approval request for a component in a project

404.description: Approval not found, or not visible / deletable by the caller.

409.description: Approval is in a terminal state (approved / rejected) and cannot be deleted. ``approval_terminal_state = true`` extension.

delete.summary: Delete a non-terminal approval (original requester, team_admin, or super_admin)

200.description: Approval found. ETag header contains the current version.

404.description: Approval not found, or not visible to the caller.

get.summary: Single approval detail — includes ETag header for optimistic concurrency

200.description: Approval transitioned. Body is the post-commit approval.

400.description: If-Match header is missing or cannot be parsed.

403.description: Caller's role is insufficient for the requested transition (under_review / approved / rejected require team_admin or super_admin).

404.description: Approval not found, or not visible to the caller.

409.description: Transition is not permitted by the state machine. ``approval_invalid_transition = true`` + ``allowed_to`` extension lists the valid next states.

412.description: If-Match version did not match the row's current version. Re-fetch the approval and retry.

patch.summary: Transition an approval's status (requires If-Match header)

get.summary: Component detail (drawer payload). 404 if component is invisible to caller.

post.description:
Open (or return the existing) automated npm remediation PR.

team_admin RBAC + opt-in enforcement live in the service. Returns 201 for a
freshly opened PR, 200 for an idempotent hit on an existing open PR, and 204
when there is nothing to remediate.

200.description: An existing open PR was returned (idempotent hit).

201.description: A new remediation PR was opened.

403.description: Caller is not a team_admin of the project's team

404.description: Project not found / not accessible

409.description: Project is not opted in to automated remediation PRs

429.description: Rate limited (too many triggers from this user) or the team's concurrent-scan cap is reached. RFC 7807 problem+json with a Retry-After header; the concurrency-cap variant adds a `limit` extension field. (The live per-team active-scan count is intentionally not exposed — see M1.)

schema.title: Min Epss

parameters.description: Tri-state reachability filter (v2.3). ``true`` → only findings whose vulnerable symbol is reachable on the call graph; ``false`` → only findings an analyser proved NOT reachable; ``unknown`` → only not-analysed findings (reachable IS NULL). Omit to disable the reachability filter.
parameters.name: reachable

schema.description: Tri-state reachability filter (v2.3). ``true`` → only findings whose vulnerable symbol is reachable on the call graph; ``false`` → only findings an analyser proved NOT reachable; ``unknown`` → only not-analysed findings (reachable IS NULL). Omit to disable the reachability filter.
schema.title: Reachable

parameters.description: Sort key. ``reachable`` ranks reachable findings first (then not-analysed, then proven-unreachable), tie-broken by severity desc.

post.description:
Cancel one of the caller's own team's scans.

PR-A1 (scan stability). Auth: any authenticated team member
(``developer`` or higher). The owning-team check lives in the service
(``cancel_scan_for_actor``) which existence-hides other teams' scans as
404 — so a developer cannot probe scan ids belonging to other teams.

Admin force-cancel (``POST /v1/admin/scans/{id}/cancel``) remains separate
and cross-team; the two share the same revoke + status-mutation core.
post.summary: Cancel a queued / running scan owned by the caller's team
Render an SCA Markdown report and (optionally) post it to a GitHub PR

get.summary: Return the caller's notification preferences (creates defaults)

put.description:
Full-row update — every channel field must be supplied.

The body's only meaningful inputs are the four channel toggles. Any
additional fields a caller may send (``user_id``, ``id``, ...) are
ignored: Pydantic strips unknown fields by default and the service is
keyed off ``actor.id``, never the body.

Chore O / M3 — In-app notifications cannot be disabled. The frontend
documents the in-app switch as "rendered but disabled"; this server-
side guard closes the API drift where a direct PUT could opt out.
put.summary: Replace the caller's notification preferences (full-row PUT)

get.summary: List the caller's connected OAuth identities (sorted oldest-first)

delete.description:
Remove an OAuth identity link from the authenticated user.

Returns 204 on success. Domain failures map to RFC 7807:

  - 404 ``urn:trustedoss:problem:oauth_identity_not_found`` —
    identity does not exist OR belongs to another user
    (existence-hide; the two cases share a shape).
  - 409 ``urn:trustedoss:problem:oauth_unlink_blocks_login`` —
    unlinking would leave the user with no way to authenticate.
delete.summary: Unlink one of the caller's OAuth identities

get.summary: Vulnerability finding drawer payload (404 if invisible to caller)

200.description: Status transitioned. Body is the post-commit detail payload.

403.description: Caller's role is insufficient (e.g. developer attempting `→ suppressed`).

404.description: Finding does not exist, or exists in a team the caller cannot access. Returned in lieu of 403 to avoid leaking existence.

409.description: if_match snapshot did not match the current updated_at.

422.description: Transition is not allowed by the workflow matrix. The Problem Details body carries an `allowed_to` extension listing the legal next states from the current status.

patch.summary: Transition a vulnerability finding's VEX status (audit-logged)
